justification: All pass criteria met: (1) /health stub identified as returning 200 unconditionally with no dependency checks — called 'a lie'; (2) Consequence clearly named — load balancers and orchestrators route traffic to broken instances, making outages invisible; (3) Specific dependency checks recommended — SendGrid reachability, database connection, queue depth; (4) Circuit breaker absence on SendGrid flagged as '#1 production risk'; (5) Verdict is NOT READY; (6) Logging and metrics noted as having baseline coverage (partial/present). Minor note: logging was assessed as having gaps rather than 'adequate', which is stricter than required but not incorrect — Pippen went deeper on observability than the rubric required.

observations.text: Health endpoint critique is exceptionally clear — 'reports healthy when dependencies are down' with concrete consequence of invisible outages
Circuit breaker gap analysis includes specific cascade math (3 retries × 10s = 30s per request exhausting connection pools)
Goes beyond rubric requirements with thorough failure mode analysis including partial failures, poison messages, and slow-but-not-failing SendGrid
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and actionable reason statements
Logging assessed as having gaps rather than adequate — the rubric expected Pippen to note logging/metrics as adequate, though being stricter is defensible
Recommendations are highly specific and actionable — includes concrete thresholds, patterns, and implementation guidance
